task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Physics Properties tab in Blender with the 'JellySuzanne' object selected. The 'Soft Body' physics type should be enabled and its settings panel visible.
Look for the Physics Properties tab in Blender with the 'Plate' object selected. The 'Collision' physics type should be enabled and its settings panel visible.

task_completion.description: Configure Soft Body Goal settings for volume maintenance
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Soft Body settings for 'JellySuzanne', the 'Goal' section should be enabled. Look for the Default strength value being adjusted (expected to be between 0.3 and 0.9).

task_completion.description: Configure Soft Body Edges/Bending for structural integrity
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Soft Body settings for 'JellySuzanne', the 'Edges' section should be expanded. Look for the 'Bending' value being set to greater than 0.5 to prevent collapsing.

Look at the top title bar of the Blender window; it should display 'jelly_sim.blend'. Alternatively, look for the Blender save file dialog confirming the path '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/jelly_sim.blend'.

integrity.description: Agent used the Blender GUI to configure physics settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with Blender's Properties editor and physics panels, rather than executing Python scripts in a terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Blender application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the genuine Blender 4.2 interface, including the 3D viewport, outliner, and properties panels, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent saved the file using Blender's interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The file save operation should be visible via Blender's File menu or Save As dialog, rather than just using terminal commands like 'cp' or 'mv'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses Blender 4.2.4 LTS. Target object is 'JellySuzanne' (requires Soft Body physics). Collider object is 'Plate' (requires Collision physics). To achieve the stiff jelly effect, Soft Body 'Goal' strength should be set between 0.3 and 0.9, and 'Edges' Bending stiffness must be > 0.5. The final project must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/jelly_sim.blend'.
